To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com

Subject: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Ava —

We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the m...
text:
From: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:12:04 -0700
Subject: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Ava —

We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
- p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
- throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing

This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.

Please treat as ...

Hi Evelyn,

We're seeing a high volume of 5xx and 429 responses against Redwood starting ...
text:
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@finvanta.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 2 May 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Elevated 5xx/429s and timeouts impacting transactions

Hi Evelyn,

We're seeing a high volume of 5xx and 429 responses against Redwood starting ~08:45 PDT this morning which is causing timeouts in our payment processing pipeline and a lot of retries on our side. Latency across affected requests has jumped from ~120ms to >2s depending on burst. Our monitoring shows our client SDK attempted exponential retries and appears to be amplifying load during the spike.

Attached: finvanta_request-traces_20260502.json — it's a small sample of traces and request IDs.

This is impacting customer transactions and the ops team is escalating. Can you advise immediate mitigations we can apply (client/server) while you investigate? ...

- Estimated steady-state QPS: 2 r/s across the three flows, with 20% peak windows that could push to 6 r/s for 5–10 minutes.

- Short annotations on plausible threat paths (e.g., cross-tenant inference data leakage, KV cache contamination, sidecar compromise) and mitigations.

The combined effect pushed our end-to-end P95 latency from ~420ms to ~1.8s and produced missing actions for some users.

- A formal confirmation if this class of failure i...

Subject: Post-demo: Medexa data retention & PII handling (next steps)

Hi Alicia — thanks again for your time this morning. Quick recap from the demo and the action items we discussed: 

- Summary: You walked us through your ingestion flow (EHR exports -> Medexa ETL -> anonymization step -> Redwood inference). Primary ask is minimizing retention of raw PII and providing fine-grained controls for audit logs.
- Questions you raised we promised to follow up on: 0-retention options for PII, what we log for debugging vs audit, and whether we can shorten logs for non-audit events.

Next steps we propose:

- Evaluation milestones (data ingest, baseline eval, accuracy check, UAT)
- Contract gating (MSA signature, DPA completion, order form sign-off)
- Procurement items (SOW/PO, billing contact, payment terms)
